claim: The text-only section (7 folios, entropy 3.9016 bits â€” highest of all sections) represents a functionally distinct register encoded with minimal or no transposition, i.e., a near-plaintext or lightly enciphered layer, while lower-entropy sections (zodiac 3.7149, astronomical 3.7471) use additional transposition steps that suppress entropy.
evidence_for: Text-only entropy 3.9016 bits is closest to natural Latin/Italian (~3.95-4.0), suggesting least transformation
Zodiac entropy 3.7149 bits is lowest, consistent with label-dominated, highly repetitive encoding adding a transposition or nulling step
Section entropy ordering (text-only > recipes > herbal > cosmological > biological > pharmaceutical > astronomical > zodiac) correlates with expected register complexity
High-confidence existing belief (0.972) already supports text-only as closest to natural language
evidence_against: Lower entropy in zodiac could reflect label brevity and small vocabulary rather than additional cipher steps
Entropy differences across sections (~0.19 bits range) are modest and could arise from topic-specific vocabulary differences without any cipher variation
The cosmological section has more folios than astronomical yet similar entropy, complicating a simple transposition-step model
test: Compute the word-type/token ratio (TTR) and word-length distribution for each section independently; run a Mann-Whitney U test comparing text-only TTR and mean word length against all other sections combined. Additionally compute section-level Zipf exponents and compare to text-only.
test_metric: Per-section TTR, mean word length, Zipf exponent, and entropy; Mann-Whitney U p-value for text-only vs. all other sections on each metric
test_threshold: Confirm structural distinctness if text-only Zipf exponent is >= 0.95 and all other sections are <= 0.90, and Mann-Whitney p < 0.05 on TTR; deny if text-only metrics fall within one standard deviation of the cross-section mean

tests_run.test: compute the word-type/token ratio (ttr) and word-length distribution for each section independently;

reasoning: If the text-only section is a distinct, less-enciphered register, it should show not just higher entropy but also different word-length distributions and a Zipf slope closer to natural language. Confirming this would justify using text-only folios as the primary decipherment target.
